strengths: 16+ years of engineering experience across distributed systems, microservices, and real-time high-throughput backends (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic: millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency DLang services)
Fintech-adjacent exposure at RIDE Capital (investment platform, TDD/CI discipline) and Optilyz (pricing optimization across multi-country configurations)
Active contributor to agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows
concerns: No evidence of executive or people-leadership experience at any level — entire career history is individual-contributor titles (Senior/Staff Developer), with no team scaling, hiring, or engineering-organization ownership
Agentic AI experience is limited to tooling and framework contribution; no demonstrated production operation of multi-agent systems with real financial or operational consequences
No exposure to core fintech infrastructure: credit risk modeling, payment flows, credit-limit management, compliance across jurisdictions, or regulatory-grade auditability requirements
response.justification: Bogdan brings strong individual-contributor credentials in distributed, high-throughput backend systems and has begun touching agentic AI tooling in his current role, but he is a poor match for this CTO position. The role demands at minimum 5 years of senior technical leadership, a track record of scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people, and demonstrated production operation of multi-agent AI systems in regulated, financially consequential environments — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His entire career is IC-level, and his AI exposure is recent and tool-building in nature rather than architectural or operational at scale. The fintech adjacency (RIDE Capital, Optilyz pricing) is too shallow to substitute for genuine credit-risk, payment-flow, or compliance infrastructure experience.
